days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: First tastes of Tokyo cuisine

timeline.title: Arrive at Tokyo Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Your flight arrives at Narita Airport. Head towards customs, then take the airport shuttle to Tokyo city center.

timeline.title: Transfer to Hotel in Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Take the Narita Express train to Shinjuku. The journey takes about 80 minutes. Enjoy views of Tokyo's suburbs along the way.

timeline.title: Check in to Shinjuku Granbell Hotel
timeline.detail: Freshen up at this modern hotel located just a 5-minute walk from Shinjuku Station. Features stylish rooms and a rooftop bar.

timeline.title: Lunch at Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Explore this vibrant seafood market and enjoy fresh sushi and seafood bowls from various vendors. A true culinary delight!

timeline.title: Explore Asakusa and Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Visit Tokyo's oldest temple, Senso-ji. Take a stroll down Nakamise Street for local snacks and souvenirs. Enjoy the atmosphere.

timeline.title: Dinner at Ikebukuro's Mutekiya
timeline.detail: Indulge in some of the best ramen in Tokyo at Mutekiya. Expect long queues for the rich, flavorful broth and handmade noodles.

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing Experience
timeline.detail: End your day by experiencing the world's busiest pedestrian crossing. Capture the hustle and bustle in a stunning night shot.

localTips: Purchase a Suica card for easy travel on trains and buses — reusable and convenient.
Try the 'shime' (end) dish in ramen places — the flavors are meant to complement your meal.
Peak dinner hours can be busy, plan to eat a little earlier to avoid long waits at popular spots.
Check out the local convenience stores for unique Japanese snacks and instant meals.
Look for vending machines with unique offerings, including hot meal options.

days.title: Cooking and Culture Day
days.subtitle: Hands-on experiences in Tokyo

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast at the hotel's restaurant featuring both Japanese and Western-style dishes.

timeline.title: Join a Sushi Making Workshop
timeline.detail: Participate in a hands-on sushi making class. Learn the art of sushi making and enjoy your creations afterward.

timeline.title: Lunch at Local Izakaya
timeline.detail: Dine at a charming izakaya. Savor various small plates, including grilled skewers and seasonal vegetable dishes.

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Take a peaceful walk through the forested paths leading to Meiji Shrine. Experience cultural tranquility in the heart of Tokyo.

timeline.title: Relax at Omotesando Hills
timeline.detail: Explore the stylish Omotesando area. Check out luxury boutiques, and cafes before relaxing at Omotesando Hills.

timeline.title: Dinner at Gonpachi Nishi-Azabu
timeline.detail: Experience traditional izakaya dining in a setting that inspired a famous film. Enjoy a mix of grilled items and delicious sake.

localTips: Make dinner reservations at popular restaurants like Gonpachi to avoid long waits.
Try different types of sushi during your workshop — each flavor has a unique story!
Send any larger items home via Japan's convenient and fast postal services — ideal for souvenirs.

hotels.name: Shinjuku Granbell Hotel
hotels.address: 2 Chome-14-5 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan

budget.note: Total for 2 nights at Shinjuku Granbell Hotel

Sample various sushi and seafood dishes
Enjoying a traditional izakaya dining experience

For 24-hour travel on local trains and subways

urgent.label: Reserve Sushi Making Workshop
urgent.note: Spots fill up quickly; book as soon as possible.
